name: Gladys Knight
abstract: Gladys Maria Knight (born May 28, 1944) is an American singer and actress. Knight recorded hits through the 1960s, 1970s, and 1980s with her family group Gladys Knight & the Pips, which included her brother Merald "Bubba" Knight and cousins William Guest and Edward Patten.

concerts.label: Rainforest Foundation Benefit Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Kidd Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: I played a lot of shows... when the singers would come in like Edie and whatever her husband’s name... and Ella Fitzgerald and Gladys Knight... I was, I do a lot of contracting
about_quotes.doc_title: Kidd Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 24, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: He said, 'Arista owns the soundtrack. They got a lot of artists, and everybody wants this song. Johnny Mathis could do it. Gladys Knight and the Pips could do it.'
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Malone
about_quotes.evidence: Gladys Knight and the Pips Christmas album. A 48-piece orchestra in the studio... And Gladys Knight is in there so they figure out what key she should do it in
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Malone interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: We flew out to California in a private plane, together with Count Basie and Gladys Knight and the Pips... playing the (?)... I mean, it was a national tour

about_quotes.speaker: Lew Soloff
about_quotes.evidence: Elton John, Billy Joel, Sting, Gladys Knight... I’ll tell you it was just thrilling. It was thrilling to play this music with these people.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lew Soloff interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: David Finck
about_quotes.evidence: I did an entire record with Gladys Knight without Gladys Knight, with a really wonderful singer who tracked for her named Vaneese Thomas.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Finck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 29, 2015
